title: SwiftRide NYC Market Entry Playbook
audience: Maya Chen, VP of Operations at SwiftRide
coreQuestion: Where should SwiftRide deploy its first 50 NYC drivers, when should they work, and how should they position against incumbents?
oneSentenceAnswer: Deploy 50 drivers across 5 zones (JFK Airport, Midtown Center, Times Square, Upper East Side, LaGuardia) with split weekday/weekend shifts, targeting 1-3 mile urban hops and airport premium runs.

slides.title: The Decision: Where to Deploy 50 Drivers in NYC
slides.content: SwiftRide Market Entry Playbook — Data-Driven Deployment Strategy

slides.title: Deploy to 5 Zones, Split by Shift, Anchor at Airports
slides.content: 50 drivers → 5 zones → 2 shifts → $560K+ monthly revenue potential

slides.title: JFK Airport Is the #1 Target — $81 Avg Fare, No Green Competition
slides.content: 137K monthly trips, $11M revenue, 3.3x citywide average fare

slides.title: Manhattan Core Offers Volume But Fierce Yellow Competition
slides.content: Midtown Center: 135K trips, $24 avg. Upper East Side: 135K trips, $20 avg.

slides.title: Green Cabs Are Weak in Core — Gap Zones Exist
slides.content: Green operates in East Harlem (12K), Queens (3K), Brooklyn (2K). Almost zero in Midtown.

slides.title: Weekday Peak: 5-6 PM With 190K+ Trips/Hour
slides.content: Two shifts: morning rush (7-10 AM) and evening peak (4-8 PM)

slides.title: Weekend Demand Shifts Later — Different Strategy Needed
slides.content: Peak 3-6 PM, strong late night (10 PM-2 AM). Weekend = entertainment market.
evidence: q7.md: Hourly Pattern by Day Type

slides.title: The Sweet Spot: 1-3 Mile Urban Hops
slides.content: 74% of trips, $10-13/mi revenue efficiency vs $5/mi for long haul

slides.title: Airports: Premium Niche, Not Core Strategy
slides.content: $75 avg fare, 8% of volume. Allocate 5-10 drivers, not 50.

slides.title: March Momentum: +11.6% Trips, +15.5% Revenue
slides.content: Market is accelerating into April. Right time to launch.

slides.title: Fastest-Growing Zones: Brooklyn, Alphabet City, Seaport
slides.content: Downtown Brooklyn +45%, Alphabet City +44%, Seaport +33% Jan→Mar

slides.title: Weekday vs Weekend: Wednesday Peak, Sunday Slowest
slides.content: Wednesday 459K trips, Sunday 306K. Plan staffing accordingly.

slides.title: Weekend Late Night: Midtown and Times Square Dominate
slides.content: 10 PM-2 AM: JFK (5K), Times Sq (3.6K), West Village (3.3K)

slides.title: The Deployment Playbook: 50 Drivers Across 3 Shifts
slides.content: Weekday: 12 morning + 8 midday + 15 evening + 5 late night. Weekend: 12 afternoon + 10 evening + 8 late night. Airport: 10 drivers split across 3 shifts.

slides.title: Risks: Weather, Competition, Driver Retention
slides.content: April weather/Easter unknown. Yellow cab retaliation possible. Driver retention critical — offer airport shifts as incentive.

slides.title: Plan B: Target Green Gap Zones
slides.content: If airports denied or Midtown too competitive: East Harlem, Forest Hills, Downtown Brooklyn, Alphabet City, Central Park.

slides.title: Next Steps: Validate, Recruit, Launch, Monitor
slides.content: 1. Validate airport authorization. 2. Recruit 50 drivers. 3. Launch in 5 zones. 4. Monitor weekly. 5. Re-run analysis in May.

caveats: Jan-Mar 2024 data only — April weather/Easter not captured
Assumes SwiftRide pricing matches TLC benchmarks
Green taxi data has higher uncertainty (56K trips/month vs 3M yellow)
Revenue estimates based on averages — actual varies by time/demand
